purpose: Standalone, integration-ready Validation & Decision Framework (VDF) for NeoGenomics IAW accessioning automation: a deterministic, configuration-driven rule engine (WHEN/DECISION/ON SUCCESS/ON FAILURE, 6 operators, 5 outcome groups) with versioned rule repository, full decision-trace explainability, in-memory/JSON + Postgres persistence, and an NL authoring copilot (linter + round-trip + dry-run + constrained LLM interpreter). Framework decides; application acts. Runtime is deterministic — no AI in the execution path; NL is authoring-time only.

goals.title: Production enterprise-grade quality
goals.description: Clean architecture boundaries, full typing, structured logging, graceful error handling, input validation, idempotency, observability, documented public APIs, XML docs on public surface.

goals.title: Security & vulnerability verification
goals.description: AuthN/AuthZ on API endpoints, input validation, parameterized data access (no injection), secrets via config/env only (never hard-coded — incl. OPENAI_API_KEY), OWASP Top 10 review, dependency vuln scans (dotnet list package --vulnerable, npm audit), no PHI/secrets in logs, constrained/schema-bound LLM output validated before use, SAST/security-review sign-off. Findings block closure.

goals.title: Rich UI/UX design quality
goals.description: The NL Rule Authoring UI must be a polished, professional experience (Fluent UI v9, WCAG 2.1 AA): NL input, generated structured rule, round-trip paraphrase, lint results, dry-run preview, governance/versioning. Use stitch / frontend-design skill for the design.

conventions.postgres: host=localhost port=5433 db=iaw user=iaw password=iaw (docker compose)
conventions.principle: Deterministic runtime; NL authoring compile-time only; compiled structured rule is the source of truth.
conventions.llm_config: OpenAI LLM is fully configurable via .env: OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL (default gpt-4o-2024-08-06 / structured-outputs capable), OPENAI_BASE_URL (default https://api.openai.com/v1), OPENAI_ENABLED. Bound via IOptions/config; never hard-coded; .env is gitignored; a .env.example is committed. Provider abstracted behind IRuleInterpreter so the model/provider can change without code edits.

features.description: M0 Contracts & Core Engine: solution scaffold; IAW.Vdf.Abstractions (rule model, vocabulary catalog, fact model, 6 operators, 5 outcome-group types, decision-trace types, integration interfaces); IAW.Vdf.Core (fact assembly, trigger engine, rule selector + dependency ordering + rule chaining, decision evaluator, outcome dispatcher with recovery, reconciler, explainability; in-memory + JSON repos). First passing regression test.
acceptance: first reference rule (PM17) evaluates correctly with trace
features.notes: The crux — built and proven first. 38 tests green.

features.description: M1 Reference rule corpus + regression harness: encode the 10 Translation-Reference rules (PM17, PM48, PM13, BL8, BL27, BL20, BL3, BL46, PM49, BL36) as JSON + fact fixtures; exercise all 5 outcome groups + recovery; cover the documented extension/exception cases (time trigger, cross-entity, multi-record, human-decision-as-fact). Runnable console demo printing decisions + traces.
features.verified_by: 108 unit tests; demo loads JSON corpus
features.notes: Corpus corrected during verification (BL46/PM13/PM49/BL36/BL20 had been authored wrong). Demo now loads JSON = single source of truth.

features.description: M2 Postgres + EF Core adapter: versioned/effective-dated rule repository tables, reference-data store, decision-trace persistence; IRuleRepository/IReferenceDataProvider EF implementations; migrations applied to Postgres (5433); integration tests against the real DB.
acceptance: EF rule repo round-trips versioned/effective-dated rules
features.notes: EF Core transitive version warning (8.0.11 vs 8.0.15) to align in M7.

features.description: M3 Authoring tooling (no LLM): structured-rule JSON schema; vocabulary linter (rejects unknown subjects/operators/outcomes, schema violations, sanity checks); round-trip paraphraser (structure->English); dry-run previewer (runs candidate rule read-only via the real evaluator over a fixture corpus). Unit-tested.
acceptance: linter rejects unknown terms + schema violations
dry-run previews matches over corpus with no side effects
features.notes: Also fixed VocabularyCatalog to contain all corpus terms; guarded by consistency test.

features.description: M4 Live LLM interpreter: IRuleInterpreter implementation calling OpenAI (Chat Completions / Responses API) with Structured Outputs (response_format json_schema) or function calling, grounded in the live vocabulary catalog; schema-validates output before returning; surfaces gaps (propose-new-term) instead of inventing; API key from env (OPENAI_API_KEY) only; provenance captured. Deterministic stub interpreter for tests/offline.
acceptance: NL compiles to structured rule using only vocabulary terms
features.verified_by: 146 offline tests + live gpt-4.1 smoke (valid rule, no invented terms)
features.notes: OpenAI gpt-4.1 via .env; validation gate enforces no-invention; runtime stays deterministic.

features.description: M5 VDF API: minimal ASP.NET Core 8 API exposing POST /evaluate (facts->outcomes+trace), authoring endpoints (interpret, lint, paraphrase, dry-run), and rule repository CRUD with governance (version/approve/effective-date/promote). AuthN/AuthZ (JWT + roles), Swagger, health, global error handling, structured logging.
features.notes: Engine wired to EF Postgres repo per-request; JWT/RBAC; fixed captive-dependency + JWT key divergence.

features.description: M6 Rich NL Authoring UI (G3): React 18 + Fluent UI v9 app — NL rule input, generated structured rule view, round-trip paraphrase, lint results, dry-run preview panel, governance (version/approve/effective-date), rule repository browser. Designed via stitch/frontend-design. WCAG 2.1 AA.
acceptance: authoring round-trip flow works against API

features.description: M7 Hardening & verification: full security review (G2) across engine/API/LLM/UI, dependency vuln scans, OWASP review, perf sanity (evaluate 100+ rules within SLA), full regression green, docs (README, architecture, integration guide, rule-authoring guide).
